get.description: Get a Workers for Platforms namespace.

schema.description: Identifier.
Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.

200.description: Get a Workers for Platforms namespace.

4XX.description: Failure to get Workers for Platforms namespace.

patch.description: Patch a Workers for Platforms namespace. Omitted fields are left unchanged.

schema.description: Identifier.
Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.

name.description: The name of the dispatch namespace.

trusted_workers.description: Whether the Workers in the namespace are executed in a "trusted" manner. When a Worker is trusted, it has access to the shared caches for the zone in the Cache API, and has access to the `request.cf` object on incoming Requests. When a Worker is untrusted, caches are not shared across the zone, and `request.cf` is undefined. By default, Workers in a namespace are "untrusted".

200.description: Patch a Workers for Platforms namespace.

4XX.description: Failure to patch Workers for Platforms namespace.

put.description: Update a Workers for Platforms namespace.

schema.description: Identifier.
Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.

name.description: The name of the dispatch namespace.

trusted_workers.description: Whether the Workers in the namespace are executed in a "trusted" manner. When a Worker is trusted, it has access to the shared caches for the zone in the Cache API, and has access to the `request.cf` object on incoming Requests. When a Worker is untrusted, caches are not shared across the zone, and `request.cf` is undefined. By default, Workers in a namespace are "untrusted".

get.description: Fetch script content from a script uploaded to a Workers for Platforms namespace.

schema.description: Identifier.
Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.
Name of the script, used in URLs and route configuration.

put.description: Put script content for a script uploaded to a Workers for Platforms namespace.

schema.description: Identifier.
Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.
Name of the script, used in URLs and route configuration.

parameters.description: The multipart name of a script upload part containing script content in service worker format. Alternative to including in a metadata part.
parameters.name: CF-WORKER-BODY-PART

parameters.description: The multipart name of a script upload part containing script content in es module format. Alternative to including in a metadata part.
parameters.name: CF-WORKER-MAIN-MODULE-PART

files.contentType: application/javascript+module, text/javascript+module, application/javascript, text/javascript, text/x-python, text/x-python-requirement, application/wasm, text/plain, application/octet-stream, application/source-map

files.description:
An array of modules (often JavaScript files) comprising a Worker script. At least one module must be present and referenced in the metadata as `main_module` or `body_part` by filename.
Possible Content-Type(s) are: `application/javascript+module`, `text/javascript+module`, `application/javascript`, `text/javascript`, `text/x-python`, `text/x-python-requirement`, `application/wasm`, `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`, `application/source-map`.

metadata.description: JSON-encoded metadata about the uploaded parts and Worker configuration.

body_part.description: Name of the part in the multipart request that contains the script (e.g. the file adding a listener to the `fetch` event). Indicates a `service worker syntax` Worker.

main_module.description: Name of the part in the multipart request that contains the main module (e.g. the file exporting a `fetch` handler). Indicates a `module syntax` Worker.

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'.

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

requestBody.description: Specify the new contents of the query.

get.description: Retrieve a saved query.

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'.

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

requestBody.description: Specify the new contents of the query.

post.description: List all the keys in your telemetry events.

datasets.description: Leave this empty to use the default datasets

filters.description: Apply filters to narrow key discovery. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

allOf.description: Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

keyNeedle.description: If the user suggests a key, use this to narrow down the list of keys returned. Make sure matchCase is false to avoid case sensitivity issues.

limit.description: Advanced usage: set limit=1000+ to retrieve comprehensive key options without needing additional filtering.

needle.description: Search for a specific substring in any of the events

requestBody.description: Find keys in your telemetry events matching a specific filter or needle.

post.description: Run a temporary or saved query.

chart.description: Whether to include timeseties data in the response

compare.description: Whether to include comparison data with previous time periods

dry.description: Whether to perform a dry run without saving the results of the query. Useful for validation

granularity.description: This is only used when the view is calculations. Leaving it empty lets Workers Observability detect the correct granularity.

ignoreSeries.description: Whether to ignore time-series data in the results and return only aggregated values

limit.description: Use this limit to cap the number of events returned when the view is events.

offset.description: Cursor pagination for event/trace/invocation views. Pass the last item's $metadata.id as the next offset.

offsetBy.description: Numeric offset for pattern results (top-N list). Use with limit to page pattern groups; not used by cursor pagination.

parameters.description: Optional parameters to pass to the query execution

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

key.description: The key to use for the calculation. This key must exist in the logs. Use the observability_keys response to confirm. Do not guess keys.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

allOf.description: Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

queryId.description: Unique identifier for the query to execute

timeframe.description: Timeframe for your query using Unix timestamps in milliseconds. Provide from/to epoch ms; narrower timeframes provide faster responses and more specific results.

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

view.description: Examples by view type. Events: show errors for a worker in the last 30 minutes. Calculations: p99 of wall time or count by status code. Invocations: find a specific request that resulted in a 500.

requestBody.description: Query your observability events, requests, and traces to build visualizations and identify insights.

post.description: List unique values found in your events.

datasets.description: Leave this empty to use the default datasets

filters.description: Apply filters before listing values. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

allOf.description: Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

needle.description: Search for a specific substring in the event.

requestBody.description: Retrieve values from your telemetry events.

put.description: Perform a complete replacement of a Worker, where omitted properties are set to their default values. This is the exact same as the Create Worker endpoint, but operates on an existing Worker. To perform a partial update instead, use the Edit Worker endpoint.

files.description:
An array of modules (often JavaScript files) comprising a Worker script. At least one module must be present and referenced in the metadata as `main_module` or `body_part` by filename.
Possible Content-Type(s) are: `application/javascript+module`, `text/javascript+module`, `application/javascript`, `text/javascript`, `text/x-python`, `text/x-python-requirement`, `application/wasm`, `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`, `application/source-map`.

metadata.description: JSON-encoded metadata about the uploaded parts and Worker configuration.

workers/message.description: Human-readable message about the version. Truncated to 100 bytes.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

keep_bindings.description: List of binding types to keep from previous_upload.

main_module.description: Name of the uploaded file that contains the main module (e.g. the file exporting a `fetch` handler). Indicates a `module syntax` Worker, which is required for Version Upload.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

schema.description: Raw javascript content comprising a Worker. Must be in service worker syntax.

files.contentType: application/javascript+module, text/javascript+module, application/javascript, text/javascript, text/x-python, text/x-python-requirement, application/wasm, text/plain, application/octet-stream, application/source-map

code.description: Code indicating that the account has exceeded the maximum number of Workers allowed.

message.description: Message explaining that the Worker limit has been exceeded and providing guidance.

x-cfLinkErrors.10014: Internal error while attempting authorization checks
x-cfLinkErrors.10015: The current account is not authorized to use workers
x-cfLinkErrors.10018: Attempted to update a script where the e-tag does not match
x-cfLinkErrors.10021: Script content failed validation checks, but was otherwise parseable

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker name is invalid.

message.description: Message explaining why the Worker name is invalid.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

key.description: Filter field name. IMPORTANT: do not guess keys. Always use verified keys from previous query results or the observability_keys response. Preferred keys: $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, $metadata.error.

value.description: Filter comparison value. IMPORTANT: must match actual values in your logs. Verify using previous query results or the /values endpoint. Ensure value type matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive unless using specific operations. Regex uses ClickHouse RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds); examples: ^5\d{2}$ for HTTP 5xx, \bERROR\b for word boundary.

code.description: Code indicating that a required URL parameter is missing.

message.description: Message explaining which required parameter is missing and suggesting to check the URL.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker request contains invalid data.

message.description: Message explaining why the Worker request is invalid, such as malformed JSON.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker name is too long to be used as a subdomain.

message.description: Message explaining that the Worker name exceeds the 63 character limit for subdomains.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker has a tag that exceeds the maximum tag length.

message.description: Message explaining why the tag is too long, including the maximum tag length.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

deployed_on.description: When the Worker's most recent deployment was created. `null` if the Worker has never been deployed.

logpush.description: Whether logpush is enabled for the Worker.

enabled.description: Whether observability is enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for observability. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%).

destinations.description: A list of destinations where logs will be exported to.

enabled.description: Whether logs are enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for logs. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%).

invocation_logs.description: Whether [invocation logs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/observability/logs/workers-logs/#invocation-logs) are enabled for the Worker.

persist.description: Whether log persistence is enabled for the Worker.

destinations.description: A list of destinations where traces will be exported to.

enabled.description: Whether traces are enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for traces. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%).

persist.description: Whether trace persistence is enabled for the Worker.

references.description: Other resources that reference the Worker and depend on it existing.

dispatch_namespace_outbounds.description: Other Workers that reference the Worker as an outbound for a dispatch namespace.

worker_id.description: ID of the Worker using the dispatch namespace.

worker_name.description: Name of the Worker using the dispatch namespace.

domains.description: Custom domains connected to the Worker.

certificate_id.description: ID of the TLS certificate issued for the custom domain.

hostname.description: Full hostname of the custom domain, including the zone name.

durable_objects.description: Other Workers that reference Durable Object classes implemented by the Worker.

namespace_id.description: ID of the Durable Object namespace being used.

namespace_name.description: Name of the Durable Object namespace being used.

worker_id.description: ID of the Worker using the Durable Object implementation.

worker_name.description: Name of the Worker using the Durable Object implementation.

queues.description: Queues that send messages to the Worker.

queue_consumer_id.description: ID of the queue consumer configuration.

workers.description: Other Workers that reference the Worker using [service bindings](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/bindings/service-bindings/).

enabled.description: Whether the *.workers.dev subdomain is enabled for the Worker.

previews_enabled.description: Whether [preview URLs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/previews/) are enabled for the Worker.

tail_consumers.description: Other Workers that should consume logs from the Worker.

updated_on.description: When the Worker was most recently updated.

adhoc.description: If the query wasn't explcitly saved

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'.

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

jwt.description: A "completion" JWT which can be redeemed when creating a Worker version.

files.description:
An array of modules (often JavaScript files) comprising a Worker script. At least one module must be present and referenced in the metadata as `main_module` or `body_part` by filename.
Possible Content-Type(s) are: `application/javascript+module`, `text/javascript+module`, `application/javascript`, `text/javascript`, `text/x-python`, `text/x-python-requirement`, `application/wasm`, `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`, `application/source-map`.

metadata.description: JSON-encoded metadata about the uploaded parts and Worker configuration.

body_part.description: Name of the uploaded file that contains the script (e.g. the file adding a listener to the `fetch` event). Indicates a `service worker syntax` Worker.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

keep_assets.description: Retain assets which exist for a previously uploaded Worker version; used in lieu of providing a completion token.

keep_bindings.description: List of binding types to keep from previous_upload.

logpush.description: Whether Logpush is turned on for the Worker.

main_module.description: Name of the uploaded file that contains the main module (e.g. the file exporting a `fetch` handler). Indicates a `module syntax` Worker.

migrations.description: Migrations to apply for Durable Objects associated with this Worker.

tags.description: List of strings to use as tags for this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

allOf.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

migrations.description: Migrations to apply for Durable Objects associated with this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

script.description: Name of the script to run if the route matches.

buckets.description: The requests to make to upload assets.

items.description: The set of assets to include in each request while uploading.
The file hash to include in this bucket.

jwt.description: A JWT to use as authentication for uploading assets.

code.description: Code indicating that a Worker with this name already exists.

message.description: Message explaining that the Worker name is already in use and suggesting to choose a different name.

annotations.description: Annotations for the Worker version. Annotations are not inherited across settings updates; omitting this field means the new version will have no annotations.

workers/triggered_by.description: Operation that triggered the creation of the version. This is read-only and set by the server.

allOf.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

logpush.description: Whether Logpush is turned on for the Worker.

migrations.description: Migrations to apply for Durable Objects associated with this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

workers_namespace-script-response.description: Details about a worker uploaded to a Workers for Platforms namespace.

dispatch_namespace.description: Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.

modified_on.description: When the script was last modified.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker has a tag containing invalid characters.

message.description: Message explaining that tags cannot contain certain characters like comma or ampersand.

region.description: Cloud region for targeted placement in format 'provider:region'.

host.description: TCP host and port for targeted placement.

region.description: Cloud region for targeted placement in format 'provider:region'.

host.description: TCP host and port for targeted placement.

target.description: Array of placement targets (currently limited to single target).

last_analyzed_at.description: The last time the script was analyzed for [Smart Placement](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/smart-placement).

workers_placement_info.description: Configuration for [Smart Placement](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/smart-placement). Specify mode='smart' for Smart Placement, or one of region/hostname/host.

workers_namespace-script-delete-bulk-res.description: Detail about bulk deletion of scripts in a namespace.

providers.description: List of cloud providers with their available regions.

workers_assets.description: Configuration for assets within a Worker.

config.description: Configuration for assets within a Worker.

_headers.description: The contents of a _headers file (used to attach custom headers on asset responses).

_redirects.description: The contents of a _redirects file (used to apply redirects or proxy paths ahead of asset serving).

html_handling.description: Determines the redirects and rewrites of requests for HTML content.

not_found_handling.description: Determines the response when a request does not match a static asset, and there is no Worker script.

oneOf.description: Contains a list path rules to control routing to either the Worker or assets. Glob (*) and negative (!) rules are supported. Rules must start with either '/' or '!/'. At least one non-negative rule must be provided, and negative rules have higher precedence than non-negative rules.
Enables routing to always invoke the Worker script ahead of all requests. When true, this is equivalent to `["/*"]` in the string array version of this field.

serve_directly.description: When true and the incoming request matches an asset, that will be served instead of invoking the Worker script. When false, requests will always invoke the Worker script.

jwt.description: Token provided upon successful upload of all files from a registered manifest.

name.description: A JavaScript variable name for the binding.

type.description: The kind of resource that the binding provides.

instance_name.description: The user-chosen instance name. Must exist at deploy time. The worker can search, chat, update, and manage items/jobs on this instance.

namespace.description: The name of the dispatch namespace.

dataset.description: The name of the dataset to bind to.

id.description: Identifier of the Hyperdrive connection to bind to.

part.description: The name of the file containing the WebAssembly module content. Only accepted for `service worker syntax` Workers.

params.description: Pass information from the Dispatch Worker to the Outbound Worker through the parameters.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint to invoke on the outbound worker.

class_name.description: Class name of the Workflow. Should only be provided if the Workflow belongs to this script.

dispatch_namespace.description: The dispatch namespace the Durable Object script belongs to.

environment.description: Optional environment if the Worker utilizes one.

namespace_id.description: Identifier of the rate limit namespace to bind to.

script_name.description: Script name that contains the Workflow. If not provided, defaults to this script name.

old_name.description: The old name of the inherited binding. If set, the binding will be renamed from `old_name` to `name` in the new version. If not set, the binding will keep the same name between versions.

version_id.description: Identifier for the version to inherit the binding from, which can be the version ID or the literal "latest" to inherit from the latest version. Defaults to inheriting the binding from the latest version.

certificate_id.description: Identifier of the certificate to bind to.

pipeline.description: Name of the Pipeline to bind to.

queue_name.description: Name of the Queue to bind to.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint to invoke on the target Worker.

service.description: Name of Worker to bind to.

index_name.description: Name of the Vectorize index to bind to.

secret_name.description: Name of the secret in the store.

store_id.description: ID of the store containing the secret.

key_base64.description: Base64-encoded key data. Required if `format` is "raw", "pkcs8", or "spki".

key_jwk.description: Key data in [JSON Web Key](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SubtleCrypto/importKey#json_web_key) format. Required if `format` is "jwk".

workflow_name.description: Name of the Workflow to bind to.

service_id.description: Identifier of the VPC service to bind to.

network_id.description: Identifier of the network to bind to. Only "cf1:network" is currently supported. Mutually exclusive with tunnel_id.

tunnel_id.description: UUID of the Cloudflare Tunnel to bind to. Mutually exclusive with network_id.

workers_binding_item.description: A binding to allow the Worker to communicate with resources.

hasStoredData.description: Whether the Durable Object has stored data.

regions.description: List of regions available for this provider.

enabled.description: Whether the Worker is available on the workers.dev subdomain.

previews_enabled.description: Whether the Worker's Preview URLs are available on the workers.dev subdomain.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

total_pages.description: The number of total pages in the entire result set.

workers_bindings.description: List of bindings attached to a Worker. You can find more about bindings on our docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/multipart-upload-metadata/#bindings.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker has exceeded the maximum number of tags allowed.

message.description: Message explaining that the tag limit has been exceeded and suggesting to remove a tag.

name.description: A JavaScript variable name for the binding.

type.description: The kind of resource that the binding provides.

key_base64.description: Base64-encoded key data. Required if `format` is "raw", "pkcs8", or "spki".

key_jwk.description: Key data in [JSON Web Key](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SubtleCrypto/importKey#json_web_key) format. Required if `format` is "jwk".

workers_secret.description: A secret value accessible through a binding.

manifest.description: A manifest ([path]: {hash, size}) map of files to upload. As an example, `/blog/hello-world.html` would be a valid path key.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

code.description: Code indicating that an observability sampling rate is invalid.

message.description: Message explaining that sampling rates must be between 0 and 1 inclusive.

entry_point.description: The entry point for the script.

workers_limits.description: Limits to apply for this Worker.

cpu_ms.description: The amount of CPU time this Worker can use in milliseconds.

cert_id.description: ID of the TLS certificate issued for the domain.

environment.description: Worker environment associated with the domain.

hostname.description: Hostname of the domain. Can be either the zone apex or a subdomain of the zone. Requests to this hostname will be routed to the configured Worker.

service.description: Name of the Worker associated with the domain. Requests to the configured hostname will be routed to this Worker.

zone_id.description: ID of the zone containing the domain hostname.

zone_name.description: Name of the zone containing the domain hostname.

workers/triggered_by.description: Operation that triggered the creation of the version.

assets.description:
Configuration for assets within a Worker.

[`_headers`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/headers/#custom-headers) and
[`_redirects`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/redirects/) files should be
included as modules named `_headers` and `_redirects` with content type `text/plain`.

config.description: Configuration for assets within a Worker.

html_handling.description: Determines the redirects and rewrites of requests for HTML content.

not_found_handling.description: Determines the response when a request does not match a static asset, and there is no Worker script.

oneOf.description: Contains a list path rules to control routing to either the Worker or assets. Glob (*) and negative (!) rules are supported. Rules must start with either '/' or '!/'. At least one non-negative rule must be provided, and negative rules have higher precedence than non-negative rules.
Enables routing to always invoke the Worker script ahead of all requests. When true, this is equivalent to `["/*"]` in the string array version of this field.

jwt.description: Token provided upon successful upload of all files from a registered manifest.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

main_module.description: The name of the main module in the `modules` array (e.g. the name of the module that exports a `fetch` handler).

migration_tag.description: Durable Object migration tag. Set when the version is deployed. Omitted if the version has not been deployed or the Worker does not use Durable Objects.

migrations.description: Migrations for Durable Objects associated with the version. Migrations are applied when the version is deployed.

modules.description:
Code, sourcemaps, and other content used at runtime.

This includes [`_headers`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/headers/#custom-headers) and
[`_redirects`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/redirects/) files used to configure 
[Static Assets](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/static-assets/). `_headers` and `_redirects` files should be 
included as modules named `_headers` and `_redirects` with content type `text/plain`.

content_type.description: The content type of the module.

number.description: The integer version number, starting from one.

source.description: The client used to create the version.

urls.description: All routable URLs that always point to this version. Does not include alias URLs, since aliases can be updated to point to a different version.

workers_compatibility_flags.description: Flags that enable or disable certain features in the Workers runtime. Used to enable upcoming features or opt in or out of specific changes not included in a `compatibility_date`.

items.description: Flag that enables or disables a specific feature in the Workers runtime.

logpush.description: Whether Logpush is turned on for the Worker.

tail_consumers.description: List of Workers that will consume logs from the attached Worker.

workers/message.description: Human-readable message about the deployment. Truncated to 100 bytes.

workers/triggered_by.description: Operation that triggered the creation of the deployment.

workers_user_limits.description: User-defined resource limits for Workers with standard usage model.

cpu_ms.description: The amount of CPU time this Worker can use in milliseconds.

new_tag.description: Tag to set as the latest migration tag.

old_tag.description: Tag used to verify against the latest migration tag for this Worker. If they don't match, the upload is rejected.

handlers.description: The names of handlers exported as part of the default export.

last_deployed_from.description: The client most recently used to deploy this Worker.

named_handlers.description: Named exports, such as Durable Object class implementations and named entrypoints.

handlers.description: The names of handlers exported as part of the named export.

name.description: The name of the exported class or entrypoint.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

compatibility_flags.description: Flags that enable or disable certain features in the Workers runtime.

cpu_ms.description: The amount of CPU time this Worker can use in milliseconds.

migration_tag.description: The tag of the Durable Object migration that was most recently applied for this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

workers_tail_consumers.description: List of Workers that will consume logs from the attached Worker.

id.description: Unique event ID. Use as the cursor for offset-based pagination.

$workers.description: Cloudflare Workers event information enriches your logs so you can easily identify and debug issues.

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.

namespace.description: Optional dispatch namespace the script belongs to.

service.description: Name of Worker that is to be the consumer.

etag.description: Hashed script content, can be used in a If-None-Match header when updating.

migration_tag.description: The tag of the Durable Object migration that was most recently applied for this Worker.

handlers.description: The names of handlers exported as part of the named export.

allOf.description: Configuration for [Smart Placement](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/smart-placement). Specify mode='smart' for Smart Placement, or one of region/hostname/host.

tag.description: The immutable ID of the script.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

target.description: Array of placement targets (currently limited to single target).

modified_on.description: When the script was last modified.

namespace_name.description: Name of the Workers for Platforms dispatch namespace.

script_count.description: The current number of scripts in this Dispatch Namespace.

trusted_workers.description: Whether the Workers in the namespace are executed in a "trusted" manner. When a Worker is trusted, it has access to the shared caches for the zone in the Cache API, and has access to the `request.cf` object on incoming Requests. When a Worker is untrusted, caches are not shared across the zone, and `request.cf` is undefined. By default, Workers in a namespace are "untrusted".

enabled.description: Whether observability is enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for incoming requests. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%). Default is 1.

destinations.description: A list of destinations where logs will be exported to.

enabled.description: Whether logs are enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for logs. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%). Default is 1.

invocation_logs.description: Whether [invocation logs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/observability/logs/workers-logs/#invocation-logs) are enabled for the Worker.

persist.description: Whether log persistence is enabled for the Worker.

destinations.description: A list of destinations where traces will be exported to.

enabled.description: Whether traces are enabled for the Worker.

head_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate for traces. From 0 to 1 (1 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%). Default is 1.

persist.description: Whether trace persistence is enabled for the Worker.

abr_level.description: The level of Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) sampling used for the query. If empty the ABR level is 1

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.

timeframe.description: Time range for the query execution

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

workers_placement_target.description: A target to run your Worker near.

workers_single_step_migrations.description: A single set of migrations to apply.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

code.description: Code indicating that the Worker does not exist.

message.description: Message explaining that the Worker was not found.

code.description: Code indicating that an unknown internal server error has occurred.

message.description: Message explaining that an unknown error occurred and providing guidance for reporting the issue.

deleted_classes.description: A list of classes to delete Durable Object namespaces from.

new_classes.description: A list of classes to create Durable Object namespaces from.

new_sqlite_classes.description: A list of classes to create Durable Object namespaces with SQLite from.

renamed_classes.description: A list of classes with Durable Object namespaces that were renamed.

transferred_classes.description: A list of transfers for Durable Object namespaces from a different Worker and class to a class defined in this Worker.

author_email.description: Email of the user who created the version.

author_id.description: Identifier of the user who created the version.

hasPreview.description: Whether the version can be previewed.

modified_on.description: When the version was last modified.

source.description: The source of the version upload.

workers_ErrorWorkerNotFound.description: Not Found - Worker does not exist.

workers_ErrorInternalServer.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected server error occurred.
